title: cipura xanthomelas subsp. xanthomelas
intro: Cipura xanthomelas subsp. xanthomelas, also known as Yellow Bells, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Cipura xanthomelas subsp. xanthomelas typically involve drying the leaves and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
